title: Cloud SQL for MySQL
aim: To create and interact with a managed MySQL instance on Google Cloud.

steps.description: Navigate to SQL in the Cloud Console and click 'Create Instance'.
Set Instance ID to 'myinstance' and provide a root password.
Select 'Enterprise' edition and a 'Development' preset for lower cost.
Click 'Create Instance' and wait for it to be ready.
Open Cloud Shell and connect to your instance.
Create a table with basic fields.
steps.command:
USE guestbook;
CREATE TABLE entries (guestName VARCHAR(255), content VARCHAR(255), entryID INT NOT NULL AUTO_INCREMENT, PRIMARY KEY(entryID));

steps.description: Insert sample data into the table.
steps.command: INSERT INTO entries (guestName, content) values ("first guest", "I got here!");

steps.description: Query the table to see your data.

commands.purpose: SQL command to create a database

viva.question: What is Cloud SQL?
viva.answer: A fully-managed database service that makes it easy to set up, maintain, manage, and administer relational databases.

result: Successfully created a managed MySQL instance and performed basic CRUD operations.
conclusion: Understood how to use managed relational database services instead of self-hosting.
